table-reference.title: Stud and fixing spacings for plywood wall cladding equal to or greater than 6.5 mm thick

row.entry: Maximum nail spacing within 1.2 m of the external corners of the building (mm) Note 1

ol.li: Maximum nail spacing using 2.8 mm or 3.5 mm galvanised clouts or flat head nails.
Fixings must be positioned a minimum of 12 mm from the edge of the sheet and not less than 50 mm from the edge of all corners.
Fasteners must penetrate not less than 30 mm into the timber frame

Wall cladding may be fixed through timber or metal battens attached to the wall frame in accordance with AS 1684.2, AS 1684.3, AS 1684.4 or NASH standard as appropriate (see fixing requirements for roof battens) so long as the minimum penetration into the wall frame is achieved.
